entries.title: Uber Co-Founder Kalanick’s Atoms Secures $1.7 Billion to Build Physical AI
entries.summary: Uber Co-Founder Travis Kalanick’s company Atoms raised $1.7 billion to develop physical artificial intelligence (AI) for all the major industrial sectors. “Mining, construction, heavy transport, food production are just a few examples of atoms-heavy industries awaiting massive digital transformation,” Kalanick said in an announcement on the company’s website. “Multiple trillion-dollar industries r

entries.title: OpenAI Unveils Product to Hone AI Voice and Chat Agents
entries.summary: OpenAI has introduced a product designed to help enterprises deploy artificial intelligence (AI) voice and chat agents for uses cases such as customer support and outbound sales. The new OpenAI Presence starts each deployment with an agent that can do one job, identifies any gaps in the agent’s abilities after it’s been launched, and proposes updates that […] The post OpenAI Unveils Product

entries.title: Dorsey’s Block Turns AI Agents Into Real Employees
entries.summary: Block on Tuesday (July 21) released Buzz, an open-source collaboration platform where employees and artificial intelligence (AI) agents work side by side in the same channels. Block CEO Jack Dorsey said on X that Buzz is “model-agnostic, decentralized, self-sovereign and open source.” He said Block built it to cut the company’s dependence on Slack and […] The post Dorsey’s Block Turns AI Age

entries.title: Ramp, FIS make AI moves
entries.summary: The two payments companies this month embarked on separate artificial intelligence initiatives, with one focused on managing AI spending and the other using the technology to mitigate cybersecurity risks.

entries.title: Visa Introduces AI Financial Assistant, Helping Banks Guide Customers from Insight to Action
entries.summary: AI Financial Assistant brings conversational financial guidance into the banking apps consumers already trust for spending and saving. The Visa Value ‑ Added Service applies benchmarking insights informed by one of the world’s largest payment networks to deliver personalized recommendations. entries.title: Bank of America embeds generative AI into customer service tools
entries.summary: Bank of America has embedded generative AI into its virtual customer service assistant EricaAssist, delivering relevant client insights in seconds to 18,000 call centre employees.

entries.title: Juspay integrates with Recurly to enable faster go-to-market for subscription businesses
entries.summary: Juspay, a global payments technology company serving enterprises and banks, today announced a partnership with Recurly, a leading subscription management and recurring billing platform. The collaboration integrates Juspay's open-source payment orchestration platform, Hyperswitch, into the Recurly ecosystem. entries.title: New Visa Data Reveals How the FIFA World Cup 2026™ Created Pop-Up Economies Across Canada, Mexico and the United States
entries.summary: New Visa data reveals the FIFA World Cup 26™ drove a nearly 20% surge in cross-border transactions across host cities in Canada, Mexico and the United States. entries.title: Kalshi Extends Its Exchange Model to GPU Pricing
entries.summary: Kalshi has launched GPU forward curves, extending its exchange infrastructure beyond prediction markets into AI compute.
